content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-high

## Summary

The amendment identifies a real prompt-context correctness bug: once explicit-ID resolution fails, reusing implicit-slot inference can fabricate adjacent plan neighbors from a slot the live bead no longer owns. Preventing that fallback in unconfirmed membership cases improves prompt accuracy and avoids misleading scope guidance.

## Votes

- `fr-3-6b46ccc3`: Accept
  `resolve_bead_plan` already distinguishes confirmed membership from weaker fallbacks, but `build_planned_elsewhere_context` currently bypasses that distinction by calling `infer_implicit_slot_hint` even after membership was not confirmed. In the explicit-ID-reassigned case, that lets a stale live bead inherit the old slot’s neighborhood and inject incorrect adjacent summaries into `prompt.md`. That is a real correctness defect, not just cosmetic output, because the generated execution prompt gives false scope cues while simultaneously saying the plan metadata is untrusted. Gating the fallback to still-implicit/title-fallback-safe slots, or suppressing adjacent neighbors when membership is unconfirmed, is the safer behavior. A regression test asserting the prompt omits those adjacent beads is warranted.
